description_long: This cowshed is a small, rectangular stone building with a gable roof made of corrugated metal sheets. The stone walls are constructed from irregularly shaped, light-colored stones, giving the structure a rustic and sturdy appearance. The front facade features a single wooden door with a dark, weathered finish and a rectangular window with a wooden frame and multiple panes. The roof extends slightly beyond the walls, providing some overhang for weather protection. The window is positioned to the right of the door, and the opposite side of the building has no visible openings. The overall design is utilitarian, typical of agricultural outbuildings found in rural or peri-urban environments.
description: A rustic stone cowshed with a corrugated metal gable roof, wooden door, and a single multi-pane window on the front facade.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden door on the left and a rectangular window with a wooden frame on the right, both set in a rough stone wall beneath a corrugated metal gable roof.
description_view_1: Side view dominated by the corrugated metal roof and stone wall, with the window visible but the door out of sight.
description_view_2: Rear gable end with only the stone wall and roof visible, no doors or windows present.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing only the stone wall and roof, with no openings or features visible.
attribute_door_style: single wooden plank door, vertically slatted, dark stained
attribute_window_presence: one rectangular window on the front facade, multi-pane, wooden frame
